description_long: This object is a rectangular fragment of carved stone, featuring a detailed relief scene with multiple human figures, some seated and others standing or kneeling. The central figure appears to be larger and more prominent, possibly representing a deity or important person, surrounded by attendants or followers. The stone has a pale, off-white coloration and a rough, unfinished edge on one side, suggesting it is a broken piece of a larger structure. The relief is deeply incised, with intricate details in the clothing, faces, and postures of the figures. The back and sides of the block are rough and uncarved, indicating it was likely intended to be viewed only from the front. The surface shows some minor chipping and wear, but the carved details remain largely intact.
description: A pale, rectangular carved stone block with a detailed relief scene of multiple human figures, featuring a prominent central figure and rough, unfinished edges.
description_view_0: The front view displays the full relief scene with several human figures, including a central seated figure and others standing or kneeling around them.
description_view_1: The side view reveals the thinness of the block and the depth of the relief carving, with little visible detail except the profile of the figures.
description_view_2: The rear view shows the back of the stone block, which is mostly rough and uncarved, with the relief scene faintly visible from behind.
description_view_3: The opposite side view again highlights the thinness of the block and the edge of the relief, with minimal detail visible.
attribute_location_context: ancient temple or historical monument site
attribute_installation_method: embedded in masonry or set atop a stone base
attribute_weathering_pattern: Minor chipping and surface wear, with some edges appearing rough and broken; no significant moss or discoloration.
attribute_presence_of_markings_or_engrav: Yes, the front face is covered with detailed relief carvings depicting human figures and decorative elements.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: Appears to be a fragment, likely once integrated into a larger carved wall or frieze.
